response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with broad full-stack, systems (DLang), and testing expertise, but does not match this role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is limited to a roughly six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project, far short of the mandated 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed, and no fixed-income or capital-markets domain background — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not investment banking. The gap on all mandatory criteria makes an interview hard to justify.
strengths: Solid low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Broad technical range across backend, tooling, and infrastructure with CI/CD and DevOps setup experience
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, none recent and none on Windows platform specified
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain expertise

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and performance-systems background
